name: SolVida Energy
peopleReachedDefinition: Estimated as households electrified (6,200) × average household size of 4.6 persons, based on DANE 2022 Colombian census data. Counts direct beneficiaries who gained new or improved electricity access through SolVida installations.
householdsReachedDefinition: Equal to the 'Households Electrified' output indicator (6,200) — one household counted per verified grid connection. Based on utility meter activation records.
jobsCreatedDefinition: Full-time equivalent (FTE) jobs directly created by SolVida operations: 95 installation technicians, 52 maintenance and operations staff, 38 sales and administrative personnel. Verified through payroll records.

outputs.label: Clean Energy Generated
outputs.methodology: Metered output from inverters, aggregated monthly

outputs.label: Households Electrified
outputs.fullName: Households with New Electricity Access (IRIS+ PI2822)
outputs.methodology: Count of unique household connections verified by utility meter activation records
outputs.source: Grid connection records and field verification

outputs.label: Solar Capacity Installed
outputs.methodology: Sum of rated capacity of all commissioned solar PV systems

outputs.label: People Reached
outputs.fullName: Total number of individuals in households with new or improved energy access
outputs.methodology: Derived from households electrified multiplied by average household size of 4.6 persons per household (Colombia DANE census data)
outputs.source: Project household survey and national census data

outputs.label: Jobs Created
outputs.fullName: Direct full-time equivalent jobs created through SolVida operations
outputs.methodology: Count of full-time equivalent positions created directly by the project, verified quarterly through payroll records
outputs.source: Company payroll records and HR database

outcomes.label: GHG Emissions Avoided
outcomes.methodology: GHG Protocol Scope 2 methodology using Colombia grid emission factors (0.126 tCO2/MWh, UPME 2024)
outcomes.source: Calculated from metered generation × grid emission factor

outcomes.label: Energy Cost Savings
outcomes.fullName: Average Energy Cost Reduction for End Users
outcomes.methodology: Comparison of average monthly energy bills pre/post installation across sample of 200 households

outcomes.label: Diesel Generators Displaced
outcomes.fullName: Fossil Fuel Generators Replaced by Solar
outcomes.methodology: Count of households that decommissioned diesel generators after solar installation, verified by field surveys
outcomes.source: Community field surveys and installation records

outcomes.label: Women Beneficiary %
outcomes.fullName: Percentage of total beneficiaries who are female
outcomes.methodology: Calculated from sex-disaggregated beneficiary registration data

financials.label: Revenue
financials.fullName: Semi-annual gross revenue from product sales and service fees
financials.methodology: Gross revenue recognized on an accrual basis per IFRS 15, reported semi-annually. Includes revenue from solar home system sales, micro-grid connection fees, and PAYGO subscription payments.
financials.source: Audited financial statements and management accounts

financials.label: Valuation
financials.fullName: Fair market value of the fund's equity position
financials.methodology: Fair market value determined using a blend of discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis and comparable transactions methodology, reviewed quarterly by the fund's valuation committee.
financials.source: Independent valuation report (annual) and management estimate (interim)

financials.label: Capital Deployed
financials.fullName: Total committed capital called and deployed into the investment
financials.methodology: Cumulative capital called from limited partners per the subscription agreement. Capital calls issued as needed to fund investment activities.

financials.label: IRR
financials.fullName: Gross internal rate of return on invested capital
financials.methodology: Time-weighted gross IRR calculated using actual cash flows (capital calls, distributions) and the latest fair market value. Computed using the XIRR method per GIPS standards.

financials.label: MOIC
financials.fullName: Multiple on invested capital (total value to paid-in)
financials.methodology: MOIC = (Current Fair Market Value + Cumulative Distributions) / Total Capital Called. Measures total value creation relative to invested capital.

financials.label: DPI
financials.methodology: DPI = Cumulative Distributions / Total Capital Called. Measures realized cash returns to investors.

financials.label: Revenue Growth
financials.methodology: Calculated as (Current Period Revenue - Prior Year Same Period Revenue) / Prior Year Same Period Revenue × 100. For periods without a prior year comparison, sequential half-over-half growth is reported.

profile.description: SolVida Energy provides affordable solar home systems, community micro-grids, and productive-use appliances to off-grid and underserved rural and peri-urban communities across Colombia. Operating primarily in the departments of Cundinamarca, Antioquia, and Valle del Cauca, the company uses a pay-as-you-go (PAYGO) model to reduce upfront cost barriers for low-income households and small enterprises.
profile.theoryOfChange: By providing affordable, clean energy access through solar home systems and mini-grids, SolVida reduces household expenditure on kerosene and diesel, improves indoor air quality, enables productive use of energy for small businesses, and reduces CO2 emissions. Increased energy access drives improvements in education (study hours), health (reduced respiratory illness), and economic activity.
profile.targetBeneficiaries: Off-grid rural households, smallholder farming communities, and micro-enterprises in the Colombian departments of Cundinamarca, Antioquia, and Valle del Cauca, with emphasis on women-headed households and communities in non-interconnected zones (ZNI).
profile.geographicFocus: Departments of Cundinamarca (rural municipalities south and east of Bogota), Antioquia (sub-regions of Bajo Cauca and Uraba), and Valle del Cauca (Pacific coast and inland rural areas). Operations span 38 municipalities with 45 distribution points.
profile.implementationApproach: Direct-to-consumer PAYGO distribution through local sales agents across Colombian departments, supported by mobile payment integration (Nequi, Daviplata) and remote monitoring IoT technology for system performance tracking.
profile.reportingFrequency: Semi-annual (H1/H2), with quarterly operational dashboards shared with fund management.
